義大利薩德尼亞奧利斯塔諾拉可尼
zh.lifeSummary: Barison I de Lacon Serra於大約1124年出生在義大利薩德尼亞奧利斯塔諾拉可尼。他和Pelegrina di Arborea至少有1個女兒。

意大利薩德尼亞奧利斯塔諾拉可尼
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Barison I de Lacon Serra于大约1124年出生在意大利薩德尼亞奧利斯塔諾拉可尼。他和Pelegrina di Arborea至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: Lacon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: locative name from Lacon in Wem (Shrops). This name has probably been confused with or absorbed by   Lakin   (1), especially in counties adjoining Shrops, such as Staffs.

2 English: while the name in Yorks could be that in (1) through migration, its appearance in late 14th-century Clint in the north-west of WR Yorks, and the persistent final -can in most early forms of the surname, suggest that it may have had a different but unknown origin.

variants.name: Laken
meanings: 1  English:  see  Lakin  .
2  English:  see  Lacon  .

variants.name: Laking
meanings: English:  see  Lacon   and   Lakin  .

variants.name: Lakin
meanings: Americanized form of Jewish Leykin (from Belarus), a metronymic from Leyke, a pet form of the Yiddish female personal name Leye, from the Hebrew female personal name Lea, from which English Leah is derived (see Genesis 29: 16) + the Slavic possessive suffix -in.
English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Lawkin, a diminutive of Law- (a pet form of the personal name Laurence; see Lawrence ). Compare Law 1 and Larkin .

nameMeanings.name: Serra
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Eduardo, Francisco, Jorge, Mario, Enrique, Jaime, Luis, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon.  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Enrico, Guido, Salvatore, Amedeo, Elio, Marco, Nicolo, Sandro, Caesar, Carlo.  Portuguese Henrique, Joaquim.

Italian, Portuguese, and Catalan: topographic name from Italian, Portuguese, Catalan serra ‘ridge or chain of hills; mountain range’ (from Latin serra ‘saw’), or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, in Italy e.g. Serra d'Aniello and Serra Pedace (Cosenza), Serra San Bruno (Vibo Valentia), and Serracapriola (Foggia), in Valencia and Catalonia (Spain) e.g. Serra d'Almos or Serra d'en Galceran.
Catalan (Serrà): topographic name for someone who lived by a sierra, from Catalan serrà, an adjective derived from serra (see 1 above), the same as Castilian Serrano .

variants.name: Serritella
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Amelio, Angelo, Carmela, Rocco, Romeo, Santa.
Italian: possibly a topographic name related to   Serra  .

variants.name: Sera
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Armando, Fernando, Jose, Alberto, Alvaro, Ana, Assunta, Elia, Enrique, Ernesto, Evelio.  Japanese Yoshio, Hideo, Hitomi, Katsumi, Kenji, Kimihiro, Masao, Mikio, Nobuhiro, Sadao, Takeo, Tsuneo.
Portuguese, Galician, and Catalan: most probably a variant of   Serra  .
Italian (Séra): from a short form of a personal name with this ending, such as Baldissera, Bonasera or Serafino .

variants.name: Serret
meanings: Catalan and southern French: topographic name from a diminutive of Catalan   Serra   and Occitan   Serre  .

variants.name: Serrano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Pedro, Carlos, Miguel, Francisco, Jesus, Jorge, Ricardo, Rafael.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Wenseslao.

Spanish: topographic name for someone who lived by a mountain ridge or chain of hills, from an adjectival derivative of serra (see Serra ). This surname is also found in the Philippines, Portugal, and Brazil.
Italian (in Calabria Serranò): habitational name for someone from any of various places called Serra (see Serra ). The surname Serrano may also be of Spanish origin.

nameMeanings.name: Barison
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Danish or Norwegian   Larsen   and Swedish   Larsson  . See also   Larrison  , compare   Lairson  .

variants.name: Larsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Nels, Niels, Sven, Thor, Knud, Nils, Per, Jorgen, Alf, Bjorn.

Danish, Norwegian, and North German: patronymic from the personal name Lars, an equivalent of Lawrence . This is also an Americanized form of the cognate Larssen. Compare Larson 1, Larrison , and Lawson 2.

variants.name: Lairson
meanings: Altered form of   Larison   or   Larrison  , surnames of Danish origin. In some cases however, it is derived directly from Swedish   Larsson   and Danish or Norwegian   Larsen  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Dev .

variants.name: Deborah
meanings: Biblical name (meaning ‘bee’ in Hebrew), borne by the nurse of Rebecca (Genesis 35:8) and by a woman judge and prophet (Judges 4–5) who led the Israelites to victory over the Canaanites. It has always been popular as a Jewish name. It was in use among Christians by the mid 16th century and was taken up by the Puritans in the 17th century, in part because the bee was a symbol of industriousness. Since then it has enjoyed enormous popularity, peaking in the 1960s. Among other famous bearers is the actress  Deborah Kerr  (  1921–2007 ).

en.lifeSummary: Barison I de Lacon Serra was born about 1124, in Laconi, Oristano, Sardinia, Italy. He had at least 1 daughter with Pelegrina di Arborea.

fr.lifeSummary: Barison I de Lacon Serra est né environ 1124, à Laconi, Oristano, Sardegna, Italie. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Pelegrina di Arborea.

de.lifeSummary: Barison I de Lacon Serra wurde um 1124 in Laconi, Oristano, Sardinien, Italien geboren. Er hatte mit Pelegrina di Arborea mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Barison I de Lacon Serra è nato il circa 1124 nel Laconi, Oristano, Sardegna, Italia. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Pelegrina di Arborea.

イタリアサルデグナオリスタノラコニ
ja.lifeSummary: Barison I de Lacon Serraは約1124年にイタリアサルデグナオリスタノラコニで生まれた。彼はPelegrina di Arboreaとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Barison I de Lacon Serra(은)는 대략 1124년 이탈리아 사르데그나 오리스탠오 라코니에서 태어났다. 그는 Pelegrina di Arborea(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Barison I de Lacon Serra nasceu aproximadamente 1124, em Laconi, Oristano, Sardegna, Itália. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Pelegrina di Arborea.

ru.lifeSummary: Barison I de Lacon Serra родился около 1124 г. в местности лакони, ористано, сардегна, Италия. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Pelegrina di Arborea.

es.lifeSummary: Barison I de Lacon Serra nació aproximadamente 1124, en Laconi, Oristano, Sardegna, Italia. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Pelegrina di Arborea.
